analysis.problem: Increased social unrest and polarization due to conflicts and political tensions
analysis.root_cause: Underlying ideological and political divisions, exacerbated by social media and biased information
leverage_points: Social media platforms and their algorithms

proposed_actions.action: Implement fact-checking and media literacy programs
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce the spread of misinformation by 20% within the next 6 months
Increase the number of joint legislative initiatives by 30% within the next year

evidence: Six-in-ten US adults say abortion should be legal in all or most cases, indicating deep divisions on social issues
Opinions on the tension between Pope Leo and President Trump split sharply along party lines, highlighting partisan polarization
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domain of Civilization, as social unrest and polarization can undermine social cohesion and destabilize institutions, ultimately affecting the planet's ecological and economic systems
analysis.summary: The data indicates increased social unrest and polarization due to conflicts and political tensions, which can be addressed through fact-checking programs and bipartisan dialogue. This problem has significant implications for the stability of human societies and the planet as a whole.

raw_items.title: Catholics in Latin America generally have positive views of Pope Leo
raw_items.summary: In six Latin American countries surveyed, most Catholics view Pope Leo XIV favorably, though his ratings trail those Pope Francis received early in his papacy.

raw_items.title: Most U.S. Catholics view Pope Leo favorably; many think Trump has been too critical of him
raw_items.summary: About eight-in-ten U.S. Catholics view Pope Leo XIV favorably, though opinions on the tension between Leo and President Trump split sharply along party lines.

raw_items.title: Is U.S. fertility at an all-time low? It depends how you measure it
raw_items.summary: U.S. fertility rates have hit historic lows, but three common measures tell different stories about whether American families are truly shrinking.

raw_items.title: What the data says about abortion in the U.S.
raw_items.summary: Six-in-ten US adults say abortion should be legal in all or most cases. Here's a look at data on abortion rates, providers, demographics and more.

raw_items.title: Bowen: US-Iran deal raises inescapable question of what the war was for
raw_items.summary: While the human cost is clear, the Iranian regime has not just survived the war, it has been empowered.

raw_items.title: Why Iran believes deal with US leaves it stronger than before
raw_items.summary: Iran has come out of the war's first chapter stronger than many expected, but its next challenge may be harder, the BBC Persian Service's Amir Azimi writes.

raw_items.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
raw_items.summary: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

raw_items.title: Thirty-five killed as gunmen attack Niger's biggest airport
raw_items.summary: Niger has been fighting a militant Islamist insurgency for a decade and in January suspected jihadists attacked the same airport.
